_comment: Curated layer of headline announced / under-construction AI megacampuses that OSM doesn't yet capture well. Coordinates are approximate campus locations drawn from the cited public sources. Maintained by the daily editorial pass: re-check status and capacity, add newly-announced campuses. capacity_mw uses the headline planned full-buildout figure where stated.

facilities.name: Stargate Site 1 (Lancium Clean Campus)
facilities.summary: Flagship site of the $500B Stargate project (OpenAI, Oracle, SoftBank), built by Crusoe on the Lancium Clean Campus. ~1.2 GW planned across eight buildings; the first went live on NVIDIA GB200 Blackwell racks in Sept 2025 (used by Oracle/OCI for OpenAI). By spring 2026 roughly four of the eight buildings were operational (~0.6 GW live by mid-2026, with the remaining buildings running behind their original schedule) and Crusoe topped out the final building, with construction of the remaining buildings now expected to run into early 2027; in June 2026 Crusoe declared the campus officially live serving Oracle and OpenAI. OpenAI/Oracle declined to expand beyond 1.2 GW; Nvidia secured the surplus and Microsoft agreed to lease ~700 MW of adjacent Crusoe capacity. The most prominent purpose-built AI training campus in the US.

facilities.name: Meta Hyperion (Richland Parish)
facilities.summary: Meta's largest data center ever — a ~4,000-acre AI campus in rural Richland Parish, powered by Meta-funded gas plants plus committed renewables, transmission and storage, supported by a ~7.5 GW Entergy Louisiana utility expansion. On July 13, 2026 Meta confirmed the full 5 GW build-out and lifted planned investment above $50B (up from an original ~$10B); it expects ~2 GW by 2030 and the full 5 GW around 2032. Construction began in earnest in early 2026 with more than $1.6B in contracts to Louisiana businesses so far; in May 2026 Louisiana granted ~$3.3B in 20-year sales-tax exemptions on data center equipment.

facilities.name: xAI Colossus
facilities.summary: Elon Musk's xAI training supercomputer on a former Electrolux site in South Memphis. Colossus 1 went live in 2024 and Colossus 2 came online in January 2026; xAI has since purchased a third building, pushing the complex toward ~2 GW and a reported ~555,000 NVIDIA GPUs (~$18B) — the largest single-site AI training installation in the world, with further expansion still under construction. Notable for on-site gas turbines drawing local controversy.

facilities.name: Microsoft Fairwater
facilities.summary: Microsoft's self-described "world's most powerful AI datacenter" on a 315-acre Mount Pleasant site, housing hundreds of thousands of NVIDIA GB200/GB300 GPUs. Microsoft announced on June 23, 2026 that the first Fairwater facility was complete and fully operational — having begun bringing equipment online in April — finishing ahead of its original schedule. A second, immediately adjacent facility is under construction (targeted 2028) and Microsoft won approval in January 2026 for ~15 additional buildings on the campus. The full campus is projected to draw toward ~3.3 GW across ~9M sq ft, with ~$4.7B in Wisconsin construction spend planned through 2028.

facilities.name: AWS Project Rainier (New Carlisle)
facilities.summary: Amazon's massive New Carlisle, Indiana campus — the compute backbone of "Project Rainier" dedicated largely to Anthropic's Claude model training. Phase 1 (500k+ custom Trainium2 accelerators) became operational in October 2025; by mid-2026 roughly 18 buildings were operating with over a dozen more under construction, Trainium3 deployment underway, and Phase 2 construction beginning — committed investment exceeds $26B, with the full campus drawing toward 2.2 GW on a 1,200-acre site.

facilities.name: Meta Prometheus (New Albany)
facilities.summary: Meta's "Prometheus" AI cluster at its New Albany, Ohio campus — expected to be the world's first ~1 GW-class AI supercomputing campus, part of Meta's "Titan Cluster" push with total investment over $10B. Built as five rapid-deployment structures with a 20-acre on-site power yard; first phases were slated to come online in early-to-mid 2026 with the cluster still building out. Powered via nuclear agreements (Oklo, TerraPower, Vistra) plus a Meta-subsidiary 200 MW on-site gas project (Sidecat) authorized by Ohio in June 2026.

facilities.name: Applied Digital 'Delta Forge 1' AI campus — Boyce (Rapides Parish)
facilities.summary: Applied Digital's ~$3.6B 'Delta Forge 1' AI Factory campus — a ~300 MW (critical IT load), ~300-acre site near Boyce in Rapides Parish, Central Louisiana ('Cenla') — is the company's first Louisiana campus. Announced alongside Gov. Jeff Landry in May 2026; site development began in January 2026 with initial operations expected mid-2027. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: Proposed Fort Bliss data center
facilities.summary: A large data center proposed near Fort Bliss, El Paso — reportedly could draw more power than the entire city. Early-stage proposal.

facilities.name: Proposed data center — near Casper
facilities.summary: A large data center proposed near Casper, Wyoming. Early-stage, surfaced via local reporting.

facilities.name: Proposed AI data center — Broadview
facilities.summary: A proposed AI data center near Broadview, Montana that would use on-site natural gas for power. Early-stage proposal.

facilities.name: Proposed $2B AI data center — Tonawanda
facilities.summary: A ~$2B AI data center proposed on a former industrial site in the Town of Tonawanda, NY. Early-stage; raising local questions.

facilities.name: Proposed data center complex — DeSoto County
facilities.summary: A massive data center complex pushed by developers in DeSoto County, Mississippi, amid community backlash. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Proposed data center campus — Upper Merion
facilities.summary: A proposed data center campus in Upper Merion Township, PA that has drawn packed, divided public meetings. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Broken Arrow
facilities.summary: A data center site under early discussion in east Broken Arrow, Oklahoma. Very early-stage.

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Inyokern
facilities.summary: The proposed RB Inyokern Data Center by R&L Capital on ~50 acres of industrial land near US-395/SR-178 in Inyokern (unincorporated Kern County), ~6 miles from Ridgecrest. A ~238,000 sq ft single-story facility with 44 diesel backup generators (~99 MW) and grid power from Southern California Edison; a California Energy Commission application was filed in late April 2026. Contested locally over projected water use.

facilities.name: Planned AI data center — Ilion (Remington site)
facilities.summary: A ~250,000 sq ft AI data center planned for the former Remington Arms site in Ilion, NY. Early-stage redevelopment proposal.

facilities.name: Proposed AI data center — Colorado Springs
facilities.summary: A proposed AI data center in Colorado Springs, Colorado, spurring debate over its merits. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Proposed data center — South Allentown
facilities.summary: A data center proposed in south Allentown, Pennsylvania. Early-stage; details still emerging.

facilities.name: Stack Infrastructure Berry Hill megacampus — Pittsylvania County
facilities.summary: Denver-based developer Stack Infrastructure's AI data center campus at the Southern Virginia Megasite at Berry Hill (Pittsylvania County, southwest of Danville). In May 2026 the Danville-Pittsylvania Regional Industrial Facility Authority unanimously approved a performance agreement under which Stack will invest at least $73.5B (a ~$100B buildout over three decades) and create ~2,050 jobs across three construction phases, with land-sale closings between June 2027 and June 2031 on ~2,990 acres — the largest private investment ever announced in Virginia.

facilities.name: Wonder Valley (Stratos Hyperscale) — Box Elder County
facilities.summary: The Wonder Valley / Stratos Hyperscale campus backed by Kevin O'Leary in rural Box Elder County (Hansel Valley), Utah — pitched at up to ~9 GW, which would make it one of the largest data centers in the world, developed with the state's Military Installation Development Authority (MIDA) as a self-powered (primarily gas) AI campus. Box Elder County commissioners approved enabling resolutions on May 4, 2026 amid fierce local opposition (a county poll found 71% opposed); in mid-May O'Leary agreed to roughly halve the ~40,000-acre footprint, with a phased buildout required. The county rejected two referendum petitions, and opponents (BEAR coalition, Alliance for a Better Utah) filed lawsuits in June 2026 seeking a public vote; commissioners also passed a 180-day moratorium on new data centers that does not affect the already-approved project. Construction is not expected before ~2028.

facilities.name: Planned data center — Inver Grove Heights
facilities.summary: A planned data center in Inver Grove Heights, MN; the city faces legal pressure over the project. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Proposed AI data center — Alaska North Slope
facilities.summary: A massive AI data center floated for Alaska's North Slope, tapping stranded North Slope energy. Highly preliminary / speculative.

facilities.name: Oracle / OpenAI data center — Saline Township
facilities.summary: A ~$16B data center campus in Saline Township (Washtenaw County), Michigan, developed by Related Digital and purpose-built for Oracle to support its OpenAI AI-compute partnership. Three single-story buildings totaling more than a gigawatt; construction began in 2026 after the township initially voted the project down.

facilities.name: Google data center — New Florence (Montgomery County)
facilities.summary: Google's $15B data center in New Florence, Montgomery County, Missouri — announced May 2026 as the largest single private investment in state history. Primarily air-cooled to minimize water use; sited along Interstate 70 west of Warrenton.

facilities.name: Hut 8 Beacon Point — Nueces County
facilities.summary: Hut 8's Beacon Point AI data center campus on a 525-acre site in Nueces County, near Corpus Christi, Texas. On July 20, 2026 Hut 8 announced a second 15-year, 352 MW IT lease with an investment-grade hyperscale tenant (~$9.8B base value), fully commercializing the 1 GW campus and bringing campus-level base-term contract value to ~$19.6B; energization is targeted for Q1 2027. In June 2026 Hut 8 closed $4.25B of investment-grade senior secured notes (Baa2, non-recourse, at project subsidiary Beacon Point DC LLC) to fund the first 352 MW phase. Has drawn local concern amid the region's water crisis.

facilities.name: Beale Infrastructure hyperscale campus — De Soto
facilities.summary: Beale Infrastructure's ~$3B hyperscale data center campus in De Soto, Kansas (about 30 miles west of Kansas City) — billed as the first hyperscale data center announced in the state. Full buildout is planned at four buildings and more than one million square feet, backed by Blue Owl Capital with power from Evergy; first phase broke ground in 2026.

facilities.name: Proposed AI data center — Pocatello (former Hoku site)
facilities.summary: A ~$2.26B (reported up to ~$2.6B), 200 MW AI campus proposed by Arizona-based Lex Developments on ~49 acres at the site of the defunct Hoku Materials polysilicon plant on the west side of Pocatello, Idaho. In May 2026 the city's hearing examiner denied the conditional-use permit after lengthy, largely negative public testimony; in June 2026 the developer's attorneys appealed, arguing the application should have been approved with conditions (e.g. additional studies) rather than rejected outright. Contested and uncertain.

facilities.name: Proposed hyperscale data center — Lakeland
facilities.summary: A hyperscale data center proposed in Lakeland, Florida. Early-stage; surfaced via local reporting.

facilities.name: Proposed $1.7B data center — Lebanon County
facilities.summary: A ~$1.7B data center proposed in Lebanon County, Pennsylvania, drawing local protest. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Central Park Commerce Center — Loxahatchee (Project Tango denied)
facilities.summary: Palm Beach County commissioners voted 5-1 on July 15, 2026 to deny (without prejudice) 'Project Tango', a proposed 3.5M sq ft, ~600 MW hyperscale AI campus in Loxahatchee, after a 12-hour hearing over noise, water and compatibility. The underlying Central Park Commerce Center approval still allows ~206,000 sq ft of data center space plus warehousing on the 202-acre site, so a much smaller facility could still be built.

facilities.name: Proposed $105M data center — Charlestown
facilities.summary: A ~$105M data center proposed in Charlestown, Indiana (Louisville metro); neighbors have raised noise and traffic concerns. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Cipher Mining 'McLennan' data center campus — Riesel (near Waco)
facilities.summary: Cipher Mining's proposed ~500 MW hyperscale data center on a ~300-acre site north of Riesel, near the Sandy Creek power plant in McLennan County, Texas (the 'second' Waco-area campus that alarmed rural residents). Developed under the code name 'McLennan' by the bitcoin miner as it pivots to AI/HPC hosting; land is secured and the site is expected to be energized around 2028, with building plans depending on the end user. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Henderson County
facilities.summary: A data center planned near a water source in Henderson County, East Texas, prompting local alarm. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Proposed $1B AI data center — Conway
facilities.summary: A ~$1B AI data center proposed near Conway, Arkansas; residents have protested over tax, water and power concerns. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Lawrenceburg
facilities.summary: A data center proposed in Lawrenceburg, Tennessee that has outraged some residents. Early-stage.

facilities.name: CoreWeave AI data center — Kenilworth
facilities.summary: An AI data center in Kenilworth (Union County), New Jersey. CoreWeave acquired the site for a planned ~400,000 sq ft, ~$1.8B facility now under construction; the project has drawn resident protest and state scrutiny over tax incentives.

facilities.name: Proposed data center campus — Archbald
facilities.summary: A data center campus proposed in Archbald, Pennsylvania (Lackawanna County). The project was removed from the state's fast-track permitting program in 2026 but remains under development. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Proposed $2B data center — Indianapolis (east side)
facilities.summary: A ~$2B data center proposed on Indianapolis's east side by a Georgia-based developer (reported as DC BLOX). In July 2026 the developer finalized downsized plans — shrinking the project days before the final city vote — citing community feedback. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Nebius AI data center campus — Independence
facilities.summary: AI-cloud provider Nebius broke ground in May 2026 on a ~400-acre AI data center campus in Independence, Missouri, amid local community opposition. Early construction stage.

facilities.name: Hut 8 River Bend AI campus — West Feliciana Parish
facilities.summary: Hut 8's River Bend AI data center campus in West Feliciana Parish, Louisiana — an up-to-$10B Phase I build drawing 330 MW of Entergy capacity to support ~245 MW of commercialized IT. Financed in April 2026 via $3.25B of investment-grade senior secured notes and leased, with Google backing, to AI-cloud firm Fluidstack for Anthropic workloads. Construction underway; operations targeted for Q2 2027.

facilities.name: Project Jupiter (Stack Infrastructure / Oracle) — Santa Teresa
facilities.summary: Project Jupiter — a ~1,400-acre AI data center campus in Santa Teresa (Doña Ana County), New Mexico near El Paso, developed by Stack Infrastructure and BorderPlex Digital with Oracle as the confirmed tenant as part of the OpenAI/Oracle Stargate initiative. Plans span four buildings with on-site gas generation, battery storage and a desalination plant; headline investment cited up to $165B. Approved by county commissioners in 2025; developers revised the power plan in 2026.

facilities.name: QTS data center campus — Van Wert
facilities.summary: QTS Data Centers is developing a ~$10B, seven-building data center campus on a 902-acre portion of a 1,500-acre 'mega site' near U.S. 30 in Van Wert County, Ohio (announced May 29, 2026). Groundbreaking is targeted for Q4 2026 with full buildout to ~2032; the campus will use a closed-loop, water-free cooling system and add ~200 permanent jobs plus ~1,500 construction jobs.

facilities.name: Approved 706-acre data center — near San Antonio
facilities.summary: A 706-acre data center development approved on the outskirts of San Antonio, Texas (reported May 2026). Operator/tenant not yet confirmed; approved but not yet under construction. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: Armory 'Innovation District' data center — St. Louis
facilities.summary: A ~$3B data center planned within the redeveloped Armory 'Innovation District' in midtown St. Louis, Missouri; the city cleared a data center permit for the project in April 2026. Early-stage redevelopment.

facilities.name: Proposed data center campus — Bessemer
facilities.summary: A proposed QTS data center campus in Bessemer, Alabama (Birmingham metro) that grew in scope through 2026; QTS confirmed its plans for the campus in July 2026. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Proposed 800-acre data center campus — Red Oak
facilities.summary: An ~800-acre data center campus proposed in Red Oak, Texas (south of Dallas), drawing packed council meetings and resident opposition in 2026. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Planned $1.1B data center campus — near Benbrook
facilities.summary: A ~$1.1B data center campus planned near Benbrook, Texas (Fort Worth area) that could break ground in summer 2026. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Project Big Pine — 500-acre data center campus, Texarkana region
facilities.summary: "Project Big Pine," a 500-acre data center campus planned for the Texarkana region on the Texas/Arkansas border. Developer not yet confirmed; early-stage.

facilities.name: Planned AI data center — Marshfield
facilities.summary: A planned AI data center near Marshfield, Missouri that drew hundreds to a packed public meeting in May 2026. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Proposed AI data center — Nodaway County
facilities.summary: A proposed AI data center in Nodaway County, Missouri raising local concern over environmental, health and utility-cost impacts. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Proposed data center — rural Johnson County
facilities.summary: A large data center proposed on hundreds of acres in rural Johnson County, Kansas — a renewed proposal reported in spring 2026 after an earlier effort. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Project Red Clay — Lowndes County (Cloverleaf Infrastructure)
facilities.summary: 'Project Red Clay' — a proposed ~800-acre AI data-center campus at the intersection of US-80 and Route 21 in rural Lowndes County, AL, developed by Cloverleaf Infrastructure. Plans call for four 720,000 sq ft buildings, a 100,000 sq ft warehouse and a 30,000 sq ft office; Cloverleaf has requested ~1,500 MW from Alabama Power and up to 100,000 gallons/day of water from the Pintlala system. The site sits just over a mile from the Robert Gardner farm on the historic Selma-to-Montgomery trail (US-80 here is the Robert Mants Memorial Highway), drawing significant community and civil-rights opposition. Packed a county meeting in April 2026; early-stage / speculative.

facilities.name: Proposed AI data center — Nassau County
facilities.summary: A proposed AI data center in Nassau County, Florida (north of Jacksonville) drawing growing local concern in mid-2026. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Proposed data center (300 acres) — Hillsboro
facilities.summary: A proposed data center tied to a ~300-acre land sale in Hillsboro, Texas that has drawn community concern. Very early-stage (land-sale phase).

facilities.name: Fisk University data center — Nashville
facilities.summary: Fisk University announced a ~$1B plan to transform its Nashville, Tennessee campus that includes building a data center. Early-stage; details still emerging.

facilities.name: Duke University data center — Durham
facilities.summary: Duke University announced plans for a new data center in Durham, North Carolina that it says will emphasize environmental responsibility and sustainability. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Google AI data center hubs — Haskell County
facilities.summary: Part of Google's $40B commitment (announced Nov 2025, running through 2027) to expand cloud and AI infrastructure in Texas — its largest investment in any state. The plan spans three new data center hubs in ERCOT's West load zone: two in Haskell County and one in Armstrong County (Panhandle). One Haskell County campus will be paired with a new solar-plus-battery storage plant to limit grid impact.

facilities.name: TeraWulf Lake Mariner AI campus — Barker
facilities.summary: TeraWulf's Lake Mariner campus — a 500 MW AI 'factory' built on ~180 acres of an 1,800-acre retired coal-plant site near Barker (Town of Somerset), New York, north of Buffalo. ~60 critical-IT MW are operational for Core42 as of early 2026, with long-term leases for 380 MW to Fluidstack; buildings CB-4 and CB-5 are slated to energize in 2026, and the site is planned to scale toward 750 MW.

facilities.name: Microsoft data center campus — La Porte
facilities.summary: Microsoft's $1B+ data center development in La Porte, Indiana. After a first campus (six buildings on ~489 acres at Radius Industrial Park), the city approved annexation and rezoning of 1,000+ acres in Pleasant Township for a second, larger campus expected to add ~11 more buildings. Site preparation began in March 2026; first buildings run through 2027 with completion targeted around 2029.

facilities.name: AWS data center campus — Wheatfield
facilities.summary: AWS plans a campus of up to nine buildings on a 304-acre site in Wheatfield (Jasper County), Indiana, adjacent to NIPSCO's Schahfer Generating Station to minimize transmission costs. The ~$7B project would be the largest in Jasper County history and AWS's third in Northwest Indiana (after New Carlisle and Hobart); it still requires county commissioner and council approval.

facilities.name: Prime Data Centers campus — Avondale
facilities.summary: Prime Data Centers broke ground in May 2026 on the first three buildings of a 240 MW data center campus in Avondale (Phoenix metro), Arizona, part of a ~$3B planned investment in the area.

facilities.name: DC Blox data center near Nashville Zoo
facilities.summary: Southeast colocation operator DC Blox's proposed data center next to the Nashville Zoo in south Nashville, TN. Revised mid-2026 site plans show the ~24-acre first building could be joined by a larger second building and an on-site substation. Heavy community pushback continues (150,000+ petition signatures, zoo opposition), and Metro Council members are weighing zoning tools to block it. Early-stage; coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: Proposed AI data center — McMinnville
facilities.summary: A high-density AI data center campus proposed near McMinnville, a Middle Tennessee city, drawing both support and calls for a moratorium among residents in 2026. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Proposed Dickerson data center
facilities.summary: A data center proposed in Dickerson (Montgomery County), Maryland, with updated site plans posted for public review in 2026. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Big Beaver
facilities.summary: A data center proposed in Big Beaver (Beaver County), Pennsylvania, that drew a large crowd to a 2026 town hall. Early-stage.

facilities.name: TAC Data Centers gigawatt campus — Wythe County
facilities.summary: Charlotte, NC-based TAC Data Centers (a subsidiary of Atlanta's The Ardent Companies) plans a 1+ GW, ~1,000-acre campus of 9–11 buildings totaling 3.5–4M sq ft in eastern Wythe County, off I-81 near the Pulaski County line. First half targeted online ~2029. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Holyoke
facilities.summary: A data center proposed in Holyoke, MA — a city with abundant municipal hydro power — drawing local controversy. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Proposed data center buildings — near Tysons
facilities.summary: "Bespoke" data center buildings proposed near residential areas of Tysons, VA (Fairfax County), drawing neighborhood opposition. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Thor Equities data center campus — near Urbana
facilities.summary: Real-estate developer Thor Equities plans a ~460,000 sq ft data center campus near Urbana, in Champaign County, Ohio. Early-stage / speculative build.

facilities.name: AI data center — Hutto
facilities.summary: A large AI data center reported under construction in Hutto, TX (northeast of Austin, Williamson County). Operator/end-tenant not yet confirmed.

facilities.name: Google data center — LaGrange
facilities.summary: Google's second data center in Georgia (after its Douglas County / Lithia Springs campus), at 411 Pegasus Parkway off I-85 in LaGrange (Troup County), ~70 miles southwest of Atlanta. Announced April 21, 2026 and already under construction; supports core Google services such as Search, Maps and YouTube.

facilities.name: Flint Hills Digital Campus — Emporia
facilities.summary: A proposed 'gigawatt-scale' data center campus west of Emporia (Lyon County), Kansas, spanning ~11 land tracts near Industrial Park IV, led by developer Kanza Park Place LLC. Early-stage: the Emporia Planning Commission set a June 23, 2026 public hearing on rezoning and a new Digital Infrastructure Overlay District. No end-tenant named; capacity is a 'gigawatt-scale' pitch, not a firm figure.

facilities.name: Duos Edge AI — Dumas Edge Data Center
facilities.summary: An operational edge data center module operated by Duos Edge AI at 421 W. 4th St in Dumas (Moore County), Texas Panhandle. A compact, water-free 'edge' colocation pod sited near the local school district, providing regional compute and connectivity for K-12 education, healthcare, carriers and enterprises; a public open house is set for June 16, 2026.

facilities.name: IREN Project Emerald — Pittsburg County (Kiowa)
facilities.summary: Australia-based IREN's proposed ~1.2 GW 'Project Emerald' AI data center campus on ~2,000 acres along U.S. Highway 69 just south of Kiowa in Pittsburg County, southeast Oklahoma, valued at about $50B across two phases. A county review committee endorsed associated tax-increment-finance districts in May 2026; IREN has bought ~340 acres so far and hopes to begin construction in 2027.

facilities.name: Eagle Rock Partners data center campus — Christian County
facilities.summary: Real-estate developer Eagle Rock Partners' proposed ~1 GW, ~$8.8B data center campus on ~475 acres of rural Christian County, Illinois (Taylorville area) — ten 100 MW buildings. Eagle Rock preps sites for future operators rather than running facilities; no formal county application has been filed and construction would not begin before 2029-2030. The county imposed a temporary data-center restriction in 2025 and residents have organized opposition.

facilities.name: Google Project Pyramid — West Memphis
facilities.summary: Google's 'Project Pyramid' data center campus on a ~1,178-acre site south of Bollinger Road in West Memphis (Crittenden County), Arkansas — five buildings supporting Google search and AI. Construction began in October 2025; Google has committed ~$4B (part of a broader ~$10B Arkansas plan, enabled by a multi-billion-dollar bond authorization) and is pairing it with a ~600 MW Entergy solar project. About 300 permanent jobs are expected — billed as the largest investment in Arkansas history.

facilities.name: Digital Realty downtown data center — 301 Virginia St
facilities.summary: Digital Realty's proposed six-story, ~380,000 sq ft network-dense colocation facility on the former Bed Bath & Beyond site at 301 Virginia St / 1930 3rd Ave in downtown Seattle. The plan, which would demolish the existing retail building, was advanced in 2026 as the City Council weighed a one-year data-center moratorium; Digital Realty describes it as 'not an AI data center.' Construction could start in 2027.

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Piedmont (Cloverleaf / Beltline)
facilities.summary: Early-stage data center proposals in Piedmont, Oklahoma (northwest of Oklahoma City) — including the 'Cloverleaf' project and a separate 'Beltline' rezoning request — that drew resident pushback over municipal water supply and growth in spring 2026. Developers tout closed-loop cooling, but the city planning commission tabled the rezonings pending a deeper study of water capacity. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: Google Meitner Energy Center — Gray County
facilities.summary: A co-located Google data center and clean-energy generation complex in the Texas Panhandle near Pampa (Gray/Roberts counties), ~60 miles northeast of Amarillo. Announced June 4, 2026 and built with Intersect Power (the clean-energy developer Google acquired in early 2026), the Meitner Energy Center pairs a hyperscale data center with 1 GW+ of on-site wind, solar and battery storage plus gas firing, so most of its power comes from dedicated generation rather than the local grid. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: Fleet Data Centers (Tract) — South Valley & Peru Ridge
facilities.summary: Fleet Data Centers, a subsidiary of Tract Capital Management, broke ground in mid-2026 on two adjacent build-to-suit campuses — South Valley and Peru Ridge — along USA Parkway in the Tahoe Reno Industrial Center (Storey County), east of Reno, Nevada. Combined ~400 MW of critical compute, part of a reported ~$10B program; the capacity is 100% pre-leased to an unnamed AA-rated, $3T+ market-cap tenant on a long-term triple-net lease. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: EdgeConneX second Bastrop County campus — Cedar Creek
facilities.summary: EdgeConneX's second data center campus in greater Bastrop County, Texas — two single-story buildings totaling ~730,000 sq ft on a ~180-acre site at 6682 FM 535 near Cedar Creek, southeast of Austin. A ~$1.4B total investment (~$700M of buildings); construction expected to begin mid-2026 with completion by the end of 2028, alongside the developer's first Cedar Creek campus already under construction. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: CyrusOne Freestone Energy Center campus — Fairfield
facilities.summary: CyrusOne broke ground in 2026 on a hyperscale data center campus adjacent to Calpine's Freestone Energy Center, a gas-fired combined-cycle plant near Fairfield in Freestone County, Texas (~90 miles south of Dallas). Calpine (a Constellation business unit) is providing 380 MW of co-located power for Phase I, with an exclusive option for a further 380 MW in Phase II; the first phase is expected operational by Q4 2026.

facilities.name: Google Port of Little Rock data center
facilities.summary: Google's proposed ~$1B data center on 380 acres at the Port of Little Rock (Sweet Home area), Arkansas — five industrial buildings totaling ~1.43M sq ft plus two office buildings and an electrical substation, drawing more than 100 MW. Google confirmed the land purchase in April 2026; as of mid-2026 the project remains in permitting, and the city was drafting data-center zoning rules that would govern it. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: Bitdeer / Geis AI data center campus — Shalersville Township
facilities.summary: A proposed 15-building AI computing campus on ~257 acres at the Turnpike Commerce Center in Shalersville Township (Portage County), Ohio, developed by Singapore-based Bitdeer in partnership with local developer Geis Cos. It would start at ~150 MW with potential to scale to ~750 MW if fully built out; construction cost is reported at over $300M excluding equipment. A local moratorium running through early November 2026 must expire or be lifted before it can proceed. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Columbus
facilities.summary: An early-stage data center proposed in Columbus, Georgia; the local Chamber of Commerce released a review of the project in spring 2026. Operator and scale not yet confirmed. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: Proposed data center — South Whitehall Township
facilities.summary: A data center proposed in South Whitehall Township (Lehigh County), Pennsylvania, near Allentown. Plans were revised in spring 2026 amid local review; operator and scale not yet confirmed. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Prichard
facilities.summary: A data center proposed in Prichard, Alabama (near Mobile), drawing mixed reactions from residents in mid-2026. Operator and scale not yet confirmed. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: Proposed data center campus near I-95 — Chesterfield County
facilities.summary: A data center campus proposed near Interstate 95 in Chesterfield County, Virginia (south of Richmond). In June 2026 the county Board of Zoning Appeals denied an appeal challenging the project, allowing it to advance. Operator and scale not yet confirmed. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: Proposed $157M West Side data center — San Antonio
facilities.summary: A proposed ~$157M data center project on the West Side of San Antonio, Texas, the subject of a public input meeting in spring 2026. Distinct from the larger 706-acre development approved on the city's outskirts. Operator not yet confirmed. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: Verrus 'Project Flex' data center — Lyon Township
facilities.summary: Proposed ~1.8M sq ft, 172-acre AI data center campus (six buildings plus a utility substation) in Lyon Township, Oakland County, developed by Verrus — a data center developer backed by Alphabet spinoff Sidewalk Infrastructure Partners — with Walbridge as general contractor. Anthropic has reportedly been in talks as an anchor tenant. The site plan won conditional approval in Sept 2025, but final permits remain pending amid strong local opposition and a March 2026 township moratorium on data centers; the developer has threatened litigation over the delays. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: Kenwood Commons data center (former Doane Stuart site) — Bethlehem
facilities.summary: Proposed hyperscale/colocation data center on the former Doane Stuart School (Kenwood) campus in the Town of Bethlehem, Albany County, as part of Guild Ventures' ~76-acre 'Kenwood Commons' mixed-use redevelopment (housing, retail and the data-center component). The owner has requested up to 180 MW. Early-stage and contested; the Albany County executive has noted the county does not control the local approval process. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: OpenAI / SB Energy 10 GW data center — Portsmouth Site (Piketon)
facilities.summary: A ~10 GW AI data center campus being developed by SoftBank's SB Energy on DOE-owned land at the Portsmouth Site (former Portsmouth Gaseous Diffusion Plant) near Piketon, Pike County, in south-central Ohio. DOE announced the partnership in March 2026 and ground was broken that month; SB Energy has committed to ~10 GW of new on-site generation (at least 9.2 GW natural gas), with a first ~800 MW data center phase targeted for early 2028 at a reported $30–40B initial cost. OpenAI is reportedly in advanced talks to lease the campus as anchor tenant, with Nvidia supplying hardware and backing the financing. Full 10 GW build-out targeted around 2030. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: Core Scientific / CoreWeave AI data center campus — Dalton (Whitfield County)
facilities.summary: An AI/HPC data center buildout led by Core Scientific (site developer) with CoreWeave as the compute tenant, anchored by an under-construction campus on Old Tilton Road (~167 acres) in Dalton, Whitfield County. In June 2026 a Whitfield County Superior Court judge validated ~$15.2B in taxable revenue bonds across four related projects, issued via the Dalton-Whitfield County Joint Development Authority with repayment left to the private companies (not taxpayers). Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: Archer Datacenters data center — Faribault
facilities.summary: A proposed ~500,000 sq ft data center on an ~84-acre site in Faribault, Rice County, developed by Archer Datacenters. On June 8, 2026 the Minnesota Court of Appeals sided with the Minnesota Center for Environmental Advocacy, ruling the city must conduct more environmental study before approving the project — which remains active but legally stalled. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: QTS AI data center campus — Clinton
facilities.summary: QTS Data Centers has proposed a multi-billion-dollar AI data center campus north of US Hwy 30 and west of Mill Creek Parkway in Clinton, Clinton County, Iowa. The city council rejected a temporary moratorium on June 9, 2026 and is drafting permitting rules, so the project is active and proceeding. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: CyrusOne data center — northern Lee County (near Moncure)
facilities.summary: A 90 MW IT-load CyrusOne data center (~300,000 sq ft on 56 acres, phase one of a 430-acre development) off Lower Moncure Road in northern Lee County, NC, developed by PointOne with local developer Helix Ventures. County commissioners approved it over the planning board's objection; construction could begin later in 2026 with operations around 2028. Coordinates approximate (near Moncure).

facilities.name: Takanock 'Project Tallmadge' data center — Strasburg
facilities.summary: Takanock has proposed 'Project Tallmadge,' a ~2-million-sq-ft data center campus on an ~80-87-acre parcel in Strasburg's Industrial Park (Shenandoah County's first data center), with construction potentially starting in late 2027. Residents have raised water and power concerns; the project remains in the proposal/review stage. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: Nscale 'Monarch Compute Campus' — Mason County (Point Pleasant)
facilities.summary: Nscale's 'Monarch Compute Campus' near Point Pleasant in Mason County, WV — on a ~2,380-acre site with 2,080 acres rezoned from agricultural to rural-industrial in May 2026 — is a planned 2 GW AI data center (scalable to 8 GW by 2031) with a state-certified on-site AI microgrid. Nscale has reported an MOU with Microsoft for 1.35 GW; an air permit is currently open for public comment. Early-stage. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: Metrobloks data center campus — Liberty
facilities.summary: Metrobloks' approved ~$1.4B, three-building (568,800 sq ft) high-density AI data center campus on ~29-30 acres near Old Hughes Road in Liberty, Clay County (Kansas City metro). The city has approved $1.4B in bonds and over $200M in tax abatements; building permits had not yet been submitted as of spring 2026, and residents have voiced opposition over land, utilities, and tax breaks. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: Tuckahoe Technology Park (Tract) — Goochland County
facilities.summary: Denver-based data center land developer Tract has filed for Tuckahoe Technology Park, a multi-phase campus of roughly 12 data center buildings on nearly 900 acres just west of West Creek Business Park in eastern Goochland County — the first project proposed under the county's new technology overlay district. In late June 2026 Tract filed a formal permit request covering ~871 acres. Tract typically prepares land/power then sells to operators. Early-stage and contested; coordinates approximate (county seat).

facilities.name: Elk River Capital data center — Elk River
facilities.summary: Developer Ned Abdul (Elk River Capital LLC) has proposed converting a vacant ~60,000 sq ft industrial building at 19178 Industrial Blvd in Elk River, MN into a roughly 33 MW data center running 24/7. The plan drew strong resident opposition at the May 2026 Planning Commission. Early-stage and contested.

facilities.name: DCIP Group AI data center campus — DeSoto County
facilities.summary: DCIP Group (CEO Jon Brown) is pursuing a gas-powered AI data center campus near Arcadia in DeSoto County, FL. The county approved rezoning ~34 acres of a decommissioned power-plant site in March 2026; the developer envisions a build-out of up to ~1,300 acres and a stated ~4,000 MW at full scale, fed by the Florida Gas Transmission pipeline. Highly speculative far-future capacity; contested by residents over water, noise and pollution.

facilities.name: Project Catalyst (Alcove Development) — Osawatomie
facilities.summary: Lawrence, KS-based Alcove Development is partnering with the City of Osawatomie on 'Project Catalyst,' a ~$1B, 500+ MW air-cooled data center campus on a 283-acre site within city limits in Miami County, KS. Officials call it the county's largest-ever economic development project; announced May 2026 and still in early permitting amid resident debate.

facilities.name: TeraWulf 'Muskie' HPC/AI campus — EastPark (Boyd County)
facilities.summary: TeraWulf acquired the ~285-acre 'Muskie' site within the 1,000-acre EastPark Industrial Park in northeastern Kentucky (near Ashland, Boyd County) for an HPC/AI hosting campus targeting more than 1 GW. The first 500 MW is slated for H2 2028 with a further 500 MW by H2 2030; it is TeraWulf's second Kentucky campus. Site acquired May 2026. Coordinates approximate (nearest city).

facilities.name: Microsoft Alviso campus — San Jose
facilities.summary: Microsoft's first purpose-built, company-owned data center in San Jose: a two-building, ~397,000 sq ft, 48 MW campus at 1657 Alviso-Milpitas Rd in the Alviso district, supporting cloud and AI demand. Ground broken June 2026 on land Microsoft has held for nearly a decade; completion targeted for 2028, with HITT as general contractor.

facilities.name: Antelope Data Center — Iron County
facilities.summary: Pronghorn Development's 'Project Antelope', a 640-acre campus approved by Iron County commissioners on June 4, 2026 (conditional use permit) after months of contested public hearings. Full buildout envisions five buildings totaling up to ~6.75M sq ft with an on-site natural gas plant of up to 1.5 GW, constructed over 8-10 years on Antelope Springs Road west of Cedar City. Coordinates approximate (nearest city).

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Spokane County (Avista MOU)
facilities.summary: An unnamed company holds a nonbinding MOU with utility Avista for a data center in Spokane County drawing 125 MW from 2029, expandable to ~500 MW by 2032 — more than half of current countywide load. Site and operator undisclosed; a community petition opposes the project and the Spokane City Council introduced a one-year moratorium on new data center permits on June 10, 2026. Coordinates approximate (city center).

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Taylor (former parkland site)
facilities.summary: A three-phase, ~$1B data center proposed in Taylor (Williamson County), starting with a ~135,000 sq ft facility on an 87-acre site the city sold to an undisclosed developer for $10M — land originally donated for use as parkland, fueling strong community opposition and a petition drive for siting restrictions. Coordinates approximate (city center).

facilities.name: Springdale data center — former Cheswick power plant site
facilities.summary: Allegheny DC Property Co., owned by investment firm Davidson Kempner, plans a 565,000 sq ft, 180 MW data center plus a 200,000 sq ft cooling/facility-management building on the 47-acre site of the demolished coal-fired Cheswick Generating Station along the Allegheny River, reportedly aimed largely at AI workloads. Borough council narrowly approved the plan in December 2025; community opposition remains active (developer open house June 10, 2026).

facilities.name: Muncy Data Center — Muncy Township
facilities.summary: Proposed ~699,574 sq ft data-center complex (three buildings: two 14-pod facilities and one eight-pod facility, plus a substation, water/sewage treatment, water tanks and stormwater basins) along John Brady Drive near Yetter Road in Muncy Township, Lycoming County, PA, about two miles east of the closed Lycoming Mall. Applicant is DANKO Holdings II/Fishlips LLC; the site-plan engineer, Justin Ross, is COO of Pennsylvania Data Center Partners, which in July 2025 announced a $15B AI-hub joint venture with PowerHouse Data Centers. A conditional-use plan was acknowledged April 28, 2026, but the township board passed a nine-month moratorium on data centers in May 2026. Early-stage / speculative.

facilities.name: AWS data center — Clinton
facilities.summary: AWS marked a $1 billion data center investment in Clinton (Hinds County), Mississippi, with a ribbon-cutting on June 9, 2026. The project retrofits the former Delphi manufacturing plant into a data center — AWS's first large-scale conversion of an existing industrial building — and is projected to create ~100 permanent jobs plus up to 1,500 construction jobs at peak.

facilities.name: Proposed AI data center — Escambia County
facilities.summary: An unnamed data center company is in active negotiations to build an AI data center in Escambia County (Pensacola area), Florida, reportedly tied to a defense-AI use. The proposal has drawn strong local opposition (a petition gathered ~2,600 signatures in four days) and city/county leaders say it is not an economic-development priority; no deal is finalized. Coordinates are the Pensacola area as an approximation pending a site. Early-stage / speculative.

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Lawrenceburg
facilities.summary: A proposed data center in Lawrenceburg (Lawrence County), Tennessee, has drawn opposition over water-quality concerns along Shoal Creek, which feeds the Tennessee River — environmental advocates warn of impacts up to 30 miles downstream in North Alabama. An online petition gained thousands of signatures. Early-stage / speculative.

facilities.name: DeepGreen Western Passage underwater AI data center — Eastport
facilities.summary: A speculative proposal by DeepGreen Western Passage SPV LLC (backed by Needham, MA real-estate agent Louis Wolfson) to sink ~34 subsea data-center pods and 170 tidal turbines into Western Passage / Passamaquoddy Bay off Eastport, powering underwater AI server racks with ~51 MW of tidal generation. A FERC preliminary-permit application was filed Feb 2026 (seeking a 48-month study window); residents have pushed the city council to oppose it over fishery and marine-heat concerns. Very early-stage; coordinates approximate (Eastport).

facilities.name: Beltline Energy proposed data center — Luther
facilities.summary: An early-stage ~400-acre data center proposal near Luther, Oklahoma (east of Oklahoma City, between N Triple X Rd and N Luther Rd) by renewable-energy developer Beltline Energy, near an OG&E power plant and tied to a proposed ~40-mile transmission line running toward Piedmont. The project has drawn heavy resident opposition over water use and utility costs; a town special-use-permit meeting was repeatedly postponed and rescheduled to June 17, 2026. Speculative / pre-filing; coordinates approximate (Luther).

facilities.name: AWS data center campus — Montgomery County (New Florence)
facilities.summary: Amazon's $10B data center campus in Montgomery County, Missouri — announced June 15, 2026, sited north of Interstate 70 just east of New Florence (distinct from Google's separately announced Montgomery County campus). Amazon pledged ~$7M in community contributions amid local opposition over water and utility costs. Coordinates approximate (near New Florence).

facilities.name: Google data center expansion — Jackson County (Bridgeport)
facilities.summary: Google's $1.5B expansion (2026–2027) of its Jackson County, Alabama data center campus, on the former TVA Widows Creek coal-plant site near Bridgeport (~40 minutes southwest of Chattanooga). The campus has operated since 2019; Google pledged to cover 100% of the electricity and grid-infrastructure costs the expansion drives and is funding a $2M TVA Energy Impact Fund and STEM grants. More than 1,000 contract workers are expected across phases. Coordinates approximate (Bridgeport).

facilities.name: AWS data center campus — Quantum Frederick (Adamstown)
facilities.summary: Amazon Web Services data center campus in TPG/Quantum Loophole's Quantum Frederick park (former Eastalco aluminum smelter site near Adamstown, Frederick County, Maryland), reported as a ~$20B investment that extends AWS's US-East (Northern Virginia) cloud region into Maryland. Maryland regulators approved the project's air-quality permit ahead of construction. Coordinates approximate (Quantum Frederick / Adamstown).

facilities.name: Infrakey AI data center campus — Ross (McLennan County)
facilities.summary: A proposed ~$10B, nearly-1 GW AI data center campus on a ~520-acre plot of unincorporated farmland next to Ross, in McLennan County north of Waco, Texas. Developed by Infrakey, a newly established AI data center developer that bought the land in 2025; backers say it would be the largest industrial development in McLennan County history (~$50M/year in tax revenue) but it has split nearby Ross and Lacy Lakeview. Distinct from Cipher Mining's separate McLennan County campus near Riesel. Early-stage; coordinates approximate (Ross town center).

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Pinellas Park (TPA Group)
facilities.summary: Boston-based developer TPA Group has proposed redeveloping an existing office-building site in Pinellas Park, Florida into a data center, demolishing the current structure to build a server facility. Smaller than the hyperscale campuses proposed elsewhere in Florida; under city review as of June 2026 amid statewide scrutiny of data center growth. Early-stage; coordinates approximate (city center).

facilities.name: Vulcan Core Bitcoin mining facility — Tyler
facilities.summary: Developer Vulcan Core has proposed a Bitcoin-mining data center at 1105 W. Erwin Street near downtown Tyler, Texas, with data center operator Barrio as the planned tenant. Sited next to an Oncor substation; the developer says it will fund its own interconnection and use a closed-loop cooling system rather than city water. Drew resident concerns over noise and water at a June 2026 community meeting; only ~7–10 full-time jobs expected. Early-stage; coordinates approximate (city center). In July 2026 the Tyler planning commission denied a permit for the proposed facility; the project's future is uncertain.

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Cave City
facilities.summary: A proposed data center near Cave City, Kentucky that the Kentucky Industrial Alliance says could bring millions in local tax revenue, discussed in June 2026. Early-stage; operator undisclosed. Coordinates approximate (city center).

facilities.name: Proposed AI data center — Lufkin
facilities.operator: AmpZ Champion Data Center Holdings / EPG Champion Development
facilities.summary: A proposed AI data center on a 1,000+ acre site in Angelina County outside Lufkin, Texas, backed by Denver-based AmpZ Champion Data Center Holdings with Atlanta-based renewable-energy partner EPG Champion Development. Planned closed-loop cooling (~30,000 gallons filled once and reused). Drew resident concerns at June 2026 county meetings; early-stage. Coordinates approximate (city center).

facilities.name: QTS data center campus — Cedar Rapids
facilities.summary: QTS data center campus near the Eastern Iowa Airport in Cedar Rapids, developed in collaboration with the City of Cedar Rapids and Alliant Energy. As of June 2026 the campus was in construction and commissioning, with QTS seeking permits for a temporary modular power plant to support test operations until grid connection (expected to cease by year-end). Coordinates approximate (near the airport).

facilities.name: Quantica Big Sky Campus — Yellowstone County
facilities.summary: Big Sky Campus — a ~5,100-acre, ~1 GW data center and on-site power generation development in Yellowstone County, Montana (about 3 miles south of Broadview and ~30 miles north of Billings), led by Quantica Infrastructure's EnCap-backed Big Sky Digital Infrastructure platform. In June 2026 Quantica signed a labor MOU with the Southeastern Montana Building and Construction Trades Council for union construction. Early-stage; coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: Crusoe AI data center — Callaway County (Fulton)
facilities.summary: Denver-based AI infrastructure company Crusoe has proposed an ~800,000 sq ft data center in North Callaway County near Fulton, MO, drawing power from Callaway Electric Cooperative and using a closed-loop cooling system. Discussed at a June 2026 community meeting and a July 2026 county open house focused on water and power questions; the exact site has not been publicly disclosed. Early-stage; coordinates approximate (Fulton city center).

facilities.name: Nebius AI factory — Oxmoor Valley (Birmingham)
facilities.summary: Amsterdam-based AI cloud provider Nebius is developing a 300 MW 'AI factory' at the former Regions Lakeshore Operations Center (201 Milan Parkway) in Oxmoor Corporate Park, Birmingham, AL. The city granted first construction permits (~$40M) in April 2026; a first 100 MW phase targets spring 2027, with a further 200 MW by fall 2028. In June 2026 Birmingham's Industrial Development Board approved ~$3.2B in tax abatements over 30 years (65% on non-education property tax, 80% on construction sales/use tax), drawing controversy. Facing resident lawsuits over zoning. Coordinates approximate (Birmingham city center).

facilities.name: Proposed AI data center — Imperial County
facilities.summary: A proposed ~330 MW AI data center campus — which would be California's largest by power and footprint — advanced by developer Sebastian Rucci in Imperial County, CA. County supervisors initially cleared the project, but amid intense opposition over water and energy use imposed a moratorium pausing all pending and future data center projects in June 2026; the developer has signaled it will challenge the county in court. Highly contested and early-stage; coordinates approximate (Imperial County).

facilities.name: Jefferson Lab research data center — Newport News
facilities.summary: The U.S. Department of Energy's Thomas Jefferson National Accelerator Facility (Jefferson Lab) broke ground in June 2026 on a ~30,000 sq ft data center in Newport News, VA, backed by ~$49M in funding, to support nuclear-physics research and high-performance computing. Purpose-built government research facility.

facilities.name: CloudBurst San Marcos Data Center I
facilities.summary: AI/HPC campus in the San Marcos-New Braunfels corridor (Guadalupe/Hays counties) master-planned for up to 1.2 GW, developed by CloudBurst Data Centers with Evolve Holdings. Ground broken November 2025; first 50 MW phase targeted for late 2026. Powered on-site via a long-term natural gas supply deal with Energy Transfer's Oasis Pipeline (enough for ~1.8 GW of generation). Tenants not yet announced.

facilities.name: Microsoft 'Project Kilby' — Reeves County (Pecos)
facilities.summary: Microsoft AI data center campus planned on 2,000+ acres near Pecos in Reeves County, West Texas (~2 GW IT capacity). Announced June 2026 alongside a 20-year power agreement with Chevron, whose subsidiary Energy Forge One (with Engine No. 1) will build an off-grid natural gas plant scaling to ~2.67 GW using GE Vernova and Solar Turbines equipment, fueled by Chevron's Permian Basin production. First power expected late 2028, with development extending through the 2030s.

facilities.name: Vantage 'Frontier' mega-campus — Shackelford County
facilities.summary: Vantage Data Centers' largest campus and investment to date: a $25B+, 1.4 GW AI mega-campus on 1,200 acres in Shackelford County, TX (near Albany), with 10 data centers totaling 3.7M sq ft supporting 250 kW+ rack densities and closed-loop liquid cooling. Construction underway with the first building slated for delivery in late 2026; Oracle has been reported as the anchor customer, with on-site gas generation planned.

facilities.name: Verrus 'Oakline at Mill Creek' — Salem
facilities.summary: Verrus's 'Oakline at Mill Creek' - a proposed ~$5.1B, three-building data center campus plus utility substation on ~75 acres in the Mill Creek Corporate Center in southeast Salem, OR, designed for cloud/AI workloads with battery storage, closed-loop cooling and grid-flexible demand. Disclosed publicly in mid-July 2026 after the city was released from an NDA; city council heard mixed public testimony on July 20, 2026 and the project is in early review (staff project ~$9M/yr to the general fund and ~75 permanent jobs). Not yet approved.

facilities.name: Proposed Blackjack Data Center — Nye County
facilities.summary: A proposed ~900-acre, ~800,000 sq ft data center campus east of Pahrump in Nye County, Nevada. Application predates the county's 2026 data center moratorium; residents have mobilized against it over Basin 162 aquifer water use, and commissioners weigh further restrictions at an August 2026 vote. A second proposal (Osprey Data Center) is also pending in the valley. Coordinates approximate (Pahrump).

facilities.name: Oppidan/CLOP data center — Cedar Rapids (33rd Ave SW)
facilities.summary: Cedar Rapids' third data center project — a ~100,000 sq ft, ~$101.5M facility at 1515 33rd Ave SW north of Highway 30, developed by CLOP Cedar Rapids IA LLC, a branch of Minnesota developer Oppidan (which built a similar Des Moines facility in 2024). Built without city incentives on industrial-zoned land; building permit issued July 2026. Coordinates approximate (city center).

facilities.name: Proposed data center — 5977 Silver Creek Valley Road, San Jose
facilities.summary: A large data center proposed to fill a vacant site at 5977 Silver Creek Valley Road in southeast San Jose, per July 2026 planning filings. Early-stage; operator undisclosed. Coordinates approximate (city center).

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Powers Ferry Place, Marietta
facilities.summary: A data center proposed on Powers Ferry Place in Marietta, Georgia (Cobb County) that drew neighborhood backlash in July 2026. Early-stage; operator undisclosed. Coordinates approximate (city center).

facilities.name: Proposed AI data center — former Crystal Geyser plant, Mount Shasta
facilities.summary: A proposed AI data center at the former Crystal Geyser bottling plant in Mount Shasta, California. Local petition and water-use concerns emerged in June 2026; early-stage. Coordinates approximate (city center).

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Rural Hall
facilities.summary: A proposed data center in Rural Hall, North Carolina (Forsyth County). The planning board recommended denial, but in June 2026 the town dropped its opposition and the town council voted to support the project. Early-stage; operator undisclosed. Coordinates approximate (town center).

facilities.name: Proposed $6B data center campus — Calvert (Mobile County)
facilities.summary: A proposed ~$6B data center campus in Calvert, Alabama (northern Mobile County) that drew questions over growth, utilities and impact in July 2026; county commissioners say they lack authority to halt it. Early-stage; operator undisclosed. Coordinates approximate (Calvert).

facilities.name: Panattoni 'Project Cornmaze' — Town of Beloit
facilities.summary: 'Project Cornmaze' — a ~$1B, four-building, 2M+ sq ft hyperscale data center campus proposed by developer Panattoni on 430+ acres at a gravel quarry spanning the towns of Beloit and Turtle in Rock County, Wisconsin. Meta has signed a pre-development agreement for the site; energy needs could reach hundreds of MW with closed-loop cooling planned. Community concern over the NDA-shrouded process; ~5-year build if approved. Coordinates approximate (Beloit).

facilities.name: Beacon 'Golden Valley Technology Hub' — Elk Hills
facilities.operator: Beacon Data Centers / California Resources Corp
facilities.summary: Canada-based Beacon Data Centers' proposed 275 MW 'Golden Valley Technology Hub' — a ~400,000 sq ft AI data center on ~100 acres of former oilfield land on the Elk Hills oil field in western Kern County, CA, developed with California Resources Corp and powered primarily by CRC's Elk Hills gas plant with closed-loop liquid cooling. Announced June 2026; early-stage. Coordinates approximate (Tupman).

facilities.name: Proposed Google data center — Hereford
facilities.summary: A proposed Google data center near Hereford in Deaf Smith County, Texas (Panhandle) — distinct from Google's Gray County project. Community informational meetings were held in July 2026; details on size and timing not yet public. Coordinates approximate (city center).

facilities.name: Crusoe/Lancium AI data center campus — Childress
facilities.summary: Crusoe and Lancium's announced 1.0 GW AI data center campus in Childress, Texas (announced July 15, 2026) — their second Texas gigawatt-scale collaboration after the Abilene Stargate campus, on a Lancium-developed grid-interactive 'Clean Campus' site. Early-stage. Coordinates approximate (city center).

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Raton
facilities.summary: A proposed data center in Raton, New Mexico (Colfax County) that drew resident pushback in June 2026. Early-stage; operator undisclosed. Coordinates approximate (city center).

facilities.name: Proposed AI data center — Yaphank
facilities.summary: A proposed AI data center in Yaphank, Long Island (Town of Brookhaven, Suffolk County, NY) facing opposition from residents and town leaders as of June 2026. Early-stage; operator undisclosed. Coordinates approximate (hamlet center).

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Cass County
facilities.summary: A proposed data center in Cass County, Nebraska (south of Omaha) drawing resident pushback over water use and farmland conversion as of July 2026. Early-stage; operator undisclosed. Coordinates approximate (Plattsmouth).

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Stafford County
facilities.summary: A data center proposed in Stafford County, Virginia on a site containing centuries-old graves, drawing preservation concerns in July 2026. Early-stage; operator undisclosed. Coordinates approximate (Stafford).

facilities.name: Diode Ventures data center — Cedar Creek Lake (Henderson County)
facilities.summary: Kansas-based Diode Ventures' proposed data center west of Cedar Creek Lake between Trinidad and Tool in Henderson County, Texas, which would draw up to 5M gallons/day from the reservoir that supplies most of Fort Worth's drinking water. Tarrant Regional Water District approval is required and residents are opposed amid drought conditions. Early-stage. Coordinates approximate (Tool).

facilities.name: University of Michigan / Los Alamos AI research data center — Ypsilanti Township
facilities.operator: University of Michigan / Los Alamos National Laboratory
facilities.summary: The University of Michigan's planned AI research computing data center in the Ypsilanti area, developed in partnership with Los Alamos National Laboratory. Facing sustained community concern; in June 2026 Rep. Debbie Dingell pressed U-M and LANL leadership to respond directly to residents. Coordinates approximate (Ypsilanti).

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Ames
facilities.summary: A data center proposed in Ames, Iowa as of June 2026. Early-stage; operator undisclosed. Coordinates approximate (city center).

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Atascosa County
facilities.summary: A proposed data center in Atascosa County, Texas (south of San Antonio) that residents were fighting as of July 2026. Early-stage; operator undisclosed. Coordinates approximate (Jourdanton).

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Bell County
facilities.summary: A proposed data center in Bell County, Kentucky facing opposition from neighboring landowners, per July 2026 reporting. Early-stage; operator undisclosed. Coordinates approximate (Pineville).

facilities.name: Planned data center — Ahwatukee (Phoenix)
facilities.summary: A planned data center in the Ahwatukee Foothills area of Phoenix, Arizona drawing neighbor concerns as of July 2026. Early-stage; operator undisclosed. Coordinates approximate (Ahwatukee).

facilities.name: TeraWulf 'Justified' Data Campus — Hawesville
facilities.summary: TeraWulf's ~750-acre 'Justified' AI/HPC data campus at the former Century Aluminum smelter in Hawesville (Hancock County), KY — a ~$3.7B project targeting ~401 MW of critical IT load using the smelter's large existing substation, with first power expected in H2 2027. In July 2026 Anthropic signed a 20-year lease for the entire campus (~$19B in contracted revenue); Fluor is engaged for preconstruction.

facilities.name: AWS 'Project Eagle' — Boling (Wharton County)
facilities.summary: Amazon Data Services has filed 'Project Eagle' — a $1.2B, four-building data center campus on ~2,700 acres near FM 1301/FM 442 in Boling, Wharton County, TX (southwest of Houston). Construction is slated to begin August 2026, with the first two buildings targeted for January 2027. Amazon has not publicly announced the project.

facilities.name: Galaxy Helios AI campus — Dickens County
facilities.summary: Galaxy Digital's 2,200+ acre Helios campus in Dickens County, West Texas — a former bitcoin mine converted to AI/HPC hosting. Phase I (133 MW of critical IT load, ~200 MW gross) was completed and handed to CoreWeave in July 2026 under a 15-year lease; CoreWeave has committed 526 MW of IT load across Phases I-III (the full 800 MW gross currently contracted). ERCOT-approved capacity totals ~1.63 GW and the site could scale to ~3.6 GW.

facilities.name: Vantage 'Lighthouse' (Oracle / OpenAI Stargate) — Port Washington
facilities.summary: Vantage Data Centers' 'Lighthouse' campus in Port Washington, WI, north of Milwaukee — a $15B+ AI data center built for Oracle and OpenAI as part of the Stargate initiative, targeted for completion in 2028. Developers assembled 1,500+ acres of farmland (at least $125M in land purchases), a buying spree that made local farmers millionaires and displaced some homeowners.

facilities.name: Site Layer 4 data center + generation campus — near Wheatland
facilities.summary: New York firm Site Layer 4 has applied to rezone 5,344 acres of the Lazy V Six Ranch northeast of Wheatland (Platte County), WY for a proposed $26B data center campus with co-located utility-scale power generation. Rezoning hearings before Platte County boards are set for August 2026. Very early-stage; coordinates approximate (Wheatland).

facilities.name: Kenmara Technology Park — Munster
facilities.summary: Saxon Partners' proposed Kenmara Technology Park data center on the former 150-acre Lansing Country Club site at Manor Avenue and Timrick Drive in Munster, IN (Chicago suburbs). More than 1,000 residents have petitioned to ban data centers in the town; the Plan Commission is weighing the proposal alongside a development ordinance that would permit data centers. Early-stage.

facilities.name: DataBank 'Project Indo' — Cartersville (Bartow County)
facilities.summary: DataBank's proposed 'Project Indo' — a 200 MW, ~1.1M sq ft data center campus on 69 acres at 218 Industrial Park Road in Cartersville (Bartow County), GA off U.S. 411, representing ~$2.4B at full build-out with a 288 MW grid connection filed. Would be DataBank's largest Atlanta-market site; first phase could open around 2032.

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Maquoketa industrial park
facilities.summary: A small-to-midsize data center campus proposed on 46 acres in Maquoketa, Iowa's south industrial park, facilitated by Dubuque development firm Gronen. Pre-development stage as of July 2026 — no operator has committed and no timeline is set; residents have protested and demanded more public input.

facilities.name: Monolith Olive Creek data center — Hallam
facilities.summary: Lincoln-based clean-hydrogen/carbon-black maker Monolith wants to host a data center at its Olive Creek campus in Hallam (Lancaster County), NE, with Crusoe eyed as project partner. Phase 1 is ~10 acres of modular buildings, with a zoning amendment and permits targeted for hearings in August-September 2026; a larger Phase 2 parcel would follow. Early-stage.

facilities.name: TeraWulf Cayuga AI campus — Lansing
facilities.summary: TeraWulf holds an 80-year ground lease on ~183 acres at the former Cayuga coal power plant on Cayuga Lake in Lansing, NY (Finger Lakes), with rights to develop up to 400 MW; ~138 MW of largely zero-carbon power could be ready as soon as 2026. Strongly contested: a ~21,000-signature petition, an Article 78 lawsuit against the zoning decision, and a possible town moratorium make the project uncertain. Coordinates approximate (Lansing).

facilities.name: AWS cloud & AI campus — Hamlet (Richmond County)
facilities.summary: Amazon's $10B cloud computing and AI 'innovation campus' in Hamlet, Richmond County, NC — announced June 2025 as one of the largest investments in state history (~500 jobs, ~800 acres, up to ~20 buildings). Amazon broke ground in 2026; July 2026 coverage examined why Richmond County welcomed the project while 30+ NC communities have adopted data-center moratoriums or restrictions.

facilities.name: Oakland University campus data center — Rochester Hills
facilities.summary: A ~20 MW AI data center proposed by developer Fairmount Properties on Oakland University's campus (relocated to Parking Lot 37), built and operated at no added cost to the university. The OU Board of Trustees voted 7-1 on June 26, 2026 to enter a formal due-diligence phase (final report expected ~June 2027) amid campus opposition.

facilities.name: Karis data center project — Dubuque County
facilities.summary: Karis was named in June 2026 as the firm behind a proposed data center project in Dubuque County, Iowa. Early-stage; coordinates are the county centroid (approximate).

facilities.name: PNK Group phased data center — Allenwood
facilities.summary: A phased data center project proposed by New York-based PNK Group off US-15 at Allenwood (Gregg Township, Union County), PA, starting with reuse of an existing industrial building. PPL has proposed ~9 miles of new 230 kV transmission across Union and Lycoming counties to serve it (construction winter 2027-2029, pending PUC approval); farmers rallied against the line in July 2026 over eminent-domain concerns.

facilities.name: Proposed data center campus — Lawrence County
facilities.summary: A proposed data center campus in Lawrence County, PA (Youngstown media market); residents packed a July 2026 public meeting to oppose it. Early-stage; coordinates are the county centroid (approximate).

facilities.name: Proposed data center — South Elgin
facilities.summary: A data center proposed in South Elgin, IL (Chicago far-west suburbs) that opponents were organizing against as of July 2026. Early-stage.

facilities.name: Proposed ~800-acre data center campus — near Grand Rapids
facilities.summary: An unidentified ('mystery') tech firm is looking to build a ~800-acre data center campus outside Grand Rapids, Michigan (reported June 2026). Very early-stage; coordinates are the Grand Rapids city centroid (approximate).

facilities.name: AVAIO $6B data center — Wrightsville
facilities.summary: AVAIO Digital Partners' proposed ~$6B data center in Wrightsville, AR (Pulaski County, southeast of Little Rock). The developer's first public community meeting in July 2026 drew sustained anger from residents over water, power and siting.

facilities.name: Proposed Amazon data center — Walla Walla County
facilities.summary: An Amazon data center proposed in Walla Walla County, WA; the county deemed Amazon's permit application incomplete in June 2026. Early-stage; coordinates are the county centroid (approximate).

facilities.name: CyrusOne sixth San Antonio campus
facilities.summary: CyrusOne announced its sixth San Antonio-area data center campus on July 20, 2026, deepening one of its largest markets. Coordinates are the city centroid (approximate); distinct from the other proposed San Antonio entries in this dataset.

facilities.name: Sabey Data Centers Umatilla campus
facilities.summary: Sabey Data Centers broke ground in July 2026 on a 714,540 sq ft data center campus in Umatilla, Oregon, extending the Umatilla/Boardman corridor anchored by AWS. Coordinates are the city centroid (approximate).

facilities.name: Planned AI data center — Hood County
facilities.summary: A new AI data center reported planned for Hood County, TX (Granbury area) in July 2026. Early-stage; coordinates are the county centroid (approximate).

facilities.name: Meta AI data center — Beaver Dam
facilities.summary: Meta's AI data center under construction in Beaver Dam, Wisconsin. On July 20, 2026 a fire damaged part of the roof during construction; no serious injuries were reported. Coordinates are the city centroid (approximate).

facilities.name: Planned $10B data center campus — near Turkey (Hall County)
facilities.summary: A ~$10B data center campus reported planned near Turkey in Hall County, in the Texas Panhandle (July 2026). Early-stage; coordinates are the town of Turkey (approximate).

facilities.name: Prometheus Hyperscale flagship campus — Evanston
facilities.summary: Prometheus Hyperscale's flagship 506-acre AI campus near Evanston (Uinta County), Wyoming — designed for 1.25 GW of initial capacity with a development path to ~5 GW, run as a fully islanded on-site natural-gas microgrid with no draw on the utility grid. Uinta County commissioners granted a unanimous conditional use permit on June 17, 2026 with 15 conditions (noise, water reporting, lighting, 5-year construction-start deadline); construction is anticipated to begin within ~6 months of final permitting with a phased 48-60 month buildout.

facilities.name: Proposed data centers — Olyphant (Triboro Commerce Park)
facilities.summary: Data center development proposed around Olyphant, PA (Lackawanna County), centered on ~1,000 acres east of the Casey Highway including Triboro Commerce Park LLC land. Borough council halted new data center development for six months in 2026 while drafting an AI data-center ordinance; Triboro filed a land-use appeal, and in June 2026 the developer criticized public hearings as unnecessary delay. Distinct from the adjacent Archbald entry.

facilities.name: Hyperscale Data (Sentinum) AI data center — Dowagiac
facilities.summary: Hyperscale Data's (formerly Ault Alliance, via subsidiary Sentinum) Dowagiac, Michigan facility — a former bitcoin-mining site being converted and expanded for AI/HPC hosting. In June 2026 the company outlined a 52 MW Michigan buildout with 20 MW contracted; the announced expansion has drawn neighbor concerns and an active lawsuit.

facilities.name: OpenAI 'Project Camellia' — Effingham County (Rincon)
facilities.summary: OpenAI's privately funded $20B 'Project Camellia' — a four-building, ~1,400-acre AI data center campus at the Savannah Gateway Industrial Hub just outside Rincon in Effingham County, near Savannah. Announced July 22, 2026 as the first data center OpenAI will design and build itself (outside the Stargate partnerships); Georgia Power will deliver ~3.2 GW in phases from 2028 to 2032. Permanent employment is expected to start around 400 and grow toward 1,000. Potentially Georgia's largest data center.

facilities.name: DC BLOX federal AI campus — Savannah River Site
facilities.summary: A ~1 GW AI data center campus with ~2 GW of dedicated on-site power generation proposed at the Department of Energy's Savannah River Site near Aiken, SC. In July 2026 the DOE's National Nuclear Security Administration selected a development consortium with DC BLOX as digital-infrastructure partner, part of the federal initiative to site AI and energy infrastructure on federal land. Early-stage; coordinates approximate (SRS northern boundary).

facilities.name: Rubix Data Centers — former AK Steel Ashland Works (Russell)
facilities.summary: Barcelona-based Submer Group's Rubix Data Centers platform proposes a ~2 GW hyperscale AI data center on the brownfield site of the former AK Steel (Armco) Ashland Works, roughly 70% within the city of Russell in Greenup County, in northeastern Kentucky — the firm's first US project, announced July 2026. Reported investment of ~$8–12B (Louisville Business First reported ~$9.6B across ~550 acres). Subject to local approvals; Rubix has told Russell officials the decision is 'ultimately up to you.' Distinct from TeraWulf's EastPark campus in adjacent Boyd County.

facilities.name: 'Perseus' data center — Pittsburg
facilities.summary: A ~99 MW data center project code-named 'Perseus' planned on the city's former municipal golf course site (slated to become a business and technology park) in Pittsburg, Contra Costa County, California — the city's first major data center. The city moved the project forward in mid-2026 amid strong resident opposition over energy, noise and water use.

facilities.name: Proposed data center development — Tucker County
facilities.summary: A data center development proposed on land in Tucker County, West Virginia (near Davis/Thomas), where advocates are pressing to learn who owns the land assembled for the project. Early-stage and locally contested. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: Proposed data center — McCormick County
facilities.summary: A data center proposed in McCormick County, South Carolina (northwest of Augusta), drawing pushback from residents. Early-stage; details still emerging. Coordinates approximate.

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Fairfield
facilities.summary: A data center proposed in Fairfield, Alabama (Birmingham metro), where a city councilor has urged residents to keep an open mind amid regional data-center controversy. Early-stage; distinct from the Nebius Oxmoor Valley and QTS Bessemer projects nearby.

facilities.name: Proposed data center — Mifflin County
facilities.summary: A data center whose plans were filed in Mifflin County, Pennsylvania in July 2026, days before a planned county moratorium vote on data-center development. Early-stage. Coordinates approximate (county seat).

facilities.name: Proposed AI data center — Limerick
facilities.summary: An AI data center proposed in Limerick Township, Montgomery County, Pennsylvania, subject of a contested July 2026 public hearing with planned protests. Early-stage. Coordinates approximate.
